title: Marriage or relationship breakdown – supporting information
description: Information to provide with your objection or private ruling request about marriage or relationship breakdown.
keywords: marriage breakdown, divorce settlement, divorce, capital gains, capital gains tax, CGT, private rulings, relationship breakdown, separation

content_blocks.text: To apply for a private ruling about a marriage or relationship breakdown:

items: complete and submit a private ruling application form
provide the supporting information listed below.

content_blocks.text: To lodge an objection about a marriage or relationship breakdown:

items: complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals
provide the supporting information listed below.

content_blocks.text: Check first whether your question is answered in Relationship breakdown and capital gains tax .
Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of the information listed below, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for a private ruling or objection about the transfer of assets upon a marriage or relationship breakdown includes:

items: which of the following occurred in relation to your marriage or relationship breakdown an order of a court or a court order made by consent under the Family Law Act 1975 or a similar law of a foreign country a maintenance agreement approved by a court under section 87 of that Act or a similar agreement under a foreign law a court order under a state, territory or foreign law relating to de facto marriage breakdowns other (provide detail)
copies of the relevant documents and information for the event listed above
full details of each of the assets transferred
the date each asset was transferred
the date of initial acquisition of the assets transferred, as well as the cost.

content_text:
## How to lodge
To apply for a private ruling about a marriage or relationship breakdown:
- complete and submit a private ruling application form
- provide the supporting information listed below.
To lodge an objection about a marriage or relationship breakdown:
- complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals
- provide the supporting information listed below.
Check first whether your question is answered in Relationship breakdown and capital gains tax .
## Supporting information
Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of the information listed below, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for a private ruling or objection about the transfer of assets upon a marriage or relationship breakdown includes:
- which of the following occurred in relation to your marriage or relationship breakdown an order of a court or a court order made by consent under the Family Law Act 1975 or a similar law of a foreign country a maintenance agreement approved by a court under section 87 of that Act or a similar agreement under a foreign law a court order under a state, territory or foreign law relating to de facto marriage breakdowns other (provide detail)
- copies of the relevant documents and information for the event listed above
- full details of each of the assets transferred
- the date each asset was transferred
- the date of initial acquisition of the assets transferred, as well as the cost.
